channel.title: Reconstruction on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in Reconstruction on Berrytree.org

item.title: James David Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. James David Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Thomas Berry × Sarah Cate — Orange County, NC → Tennessee
Gen 4David Berry × Elizabeth Shipman — Tennessee
Gen 5Isaac Berry × Amanda Josephine Blackwood — Wayne County, Tennessee
Gen 6James David Berry × Millissa Harlow — Wayne County, Tennessee

Generation 6 James David Berry

Born
September 15, 1865, Wayne County, Tennessee
Died
May 30, 1930, Wayne County, Tennessee
Married
Millissa Harlow (married c. 1888, died November 6, 1914)
Parents
Isaac Berry and Amanda Josephine Blackwood Berry, Wayne County, Tennessee
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of James David Berry#

Jim Dave Berry was born in Wayne County Tennessee on the 15th Day of September 1865 He was the first child of Isaac and Amanda Josephine Blackwood Berry. The picture at the top of this page is Jim Dave Berry’s house. The people in the picture in front of Jim Dave’s house are from left to right are: Farm Hand: Robert Benson, Ode Berry, Ernest John Berry, Thelma Berry, Emma Lee Stutts Berry, Obie Berry, Jim Dave Berry, and Cove Berry.

item.title: Kate Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Kate Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Lewis Berry × Sally Toler — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 5Ira Berry × Nancy Wynn — NC and Kentucky
Gen 6Franklin Pierce Berry × Sophie Sellers — Kentucky
Gen 7Kate Berry × Benjamin Franklin Moake — Kentucky → Williamson County, Illinois

Generation 7 Kate Berry

Born
August 12, 1896, Sebree, Webster County, Kentucky
Died
August 22, 1966, Williamson County, Illinois
Married
Benjamin Franklin Moake (born August 18, 1892, died February 9, 1979)
Parents
Franklin Pierce Berry and Sophia M. Sellers Berry, Kentucky
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Kate Berry#

Kate Berry Was born Aug 12, 1896 Sebree, Webster Co., Ky, she died Aug 22, 1966 and is buried at Lake Creek Cemetery, Williamson Co., Illinois. Kate married Benjamin Franklin Moake in Williamson Co, Illinois, son of Eziekel Moake and Dell Everett. He was born August 18, 1892 in Lake Creek, Williamson Co., Illinois, and died February 09, 1979 in Williamson Co., Illinois. In 1941, Kate had her birth certificate replaced, the witnesses for her were A.M. Sellers, an uncle age 75, Samuel Hansford husband of Rebecca Sellers and G.W. Bronson, a non relative age 80 years. The certificate is listed as #175914 in the bureau of vital statistics, Commonwealth of Kentucky, Webster Co.

item.title: Mary Leona Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mary Leona Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 4David Middleton Berry × Didama Box — Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 5Alfred W. Berry × Nancy Crowley — Fayette County, Alabama
Gen 6Mary Leona Berry × Monroe C. Berry — Alabama

Generation 6 Mary Leona Berry

Born
c. 1868, Alabama
Died
April 5, 1949, Jefferson, Alabama
Married
Monroe C. Berry (married November 5, 1889, died 1942)
Parents
Alfred W. Berry and Nancy Crowley Berry, Alabama
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Mary Leona Berry#

Mary Leona Berry was born about 1868 in Alabama and she died on Tuesday April 5, 1949 in Jefferson, Alabama. Mary Leona married Monroe C. Berry on November 5th, 1889. Yes this is a Berry who married a Berry. Monroe was born about 1866 in Alabama and died in 1942 in Jefferson, Alabama.

item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Ode Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Thomas Berry × Sarah Cate — Orange County, NC → Tennessee
Gen 4David Berry × Elizabeth Shipman — Tennessee
Gen 5Isaac Berry × Amanda Josephine Blackwood — Wayne County, Tennessee
Gen 6James David Berry × Millissa Harlow — Wayne County, Tennessee
Gen 7Ode Berry × Emma Lee Stuits — Wayne County, Tennessee

Generation 7 Ode Berry

Born
December 2, 1888, Wayne County, Tennessee
Died
February 8, 1951
Married
Emma Lee Stuits (married December 26, 1909, died October 14, 1979)
Parents
James David Berry and Millissa Harlow Berry, Wayne County, Tennessee
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Ode Berry#

Ode Berry was born in Wayne County Tennessee on December 2, 1888 and died on February 8 1951 He was the first child of James David and Millissa Harlow to reach adulthood. Ode married Emma Lee Stuits on December 26, 1909. Emma Lee Stuits was born on September 9th 1890 and died on October the 14th 1979.

September 8, 1869, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
May 12, 1965, Orange County, North Carolina
Married

Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Wiley P. Berry#

The Berry family home, built by Wiley P. Berry on 100 acres given to him by his father John Robert Berry

item.title: Chapter 15: John Robert Berry 1842–1917
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 14: Thomas Person Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 16: Wiley P. Berry →

Chapter 15#

John Robert Berry 1842–1917#

John Robert Berry was born on September 25th, 1842, and was the third son of Thomas Person and Sarah Lunsford Berry. He is also my great-grandfather. He fought in the Civil War and was wounded in the battle of Cold Harbor. After the war, he returned to Orange County, North Carolina, and married Elizabeth Frances Bowling on November 30th, 1865.

item.title: Chapter 16: Wiley P. Berry 1869–1965
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 15: John Robert Berry | Table of Contents | Chapter 17: Y-DNA Testing →

Chapter 16#

Wiley P. Berry 1869–1965#

Wiley P. Berry was the third child of John Robert and Elizabeth Frances Bowling Berry.

item.description:
1. Home

Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — NC and Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 6 Mattie Lee Berry Strickland

Born
1882, Fayette County, Alabama
Died
1916, Berry Cemetery, Fayette County, Alabama
Married
David Loyd Strickland — married c. 1902
Parents
Henry Bradford Berry and Malissa Jeffries
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Lineage#

  1. Gen 1 — Robert and Mary Williamson Berry — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Gen 2 — Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry — Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Gen 3 — Robert Jr. and Mary Waggoner Berry — NC and Fayette County, Alabama
  4. Gen 4 — Thompson and Roseann Gutman Berry — Fayette County, Alabama
  5. Gen 5 — Henry Bradford and Malissa Jeffries Berry

The Story of Mattie Lee Berry#

Mattie Lee Berry was born 1882 in Fayette County, Alabama. She married David Loyd Strickland about 1902. In 1910 Mattie Lee Berry Strickland and her family were living with her widowed mother Malissa Jeffries Berry in Holly Springs, Fayette County, Alabama.

Willie Howell Berry was born on February 22, 1882 in Henrietta, Clay County, Texas. He died on November 7, 1958 in Amherst, Lamb County, Texas. Willie Howell Berry and Sarah May Benham were married on February 21, 1909. Sarah May Benham was born on April 29, 1882 in Bloomfield, Indiana and she died on October 22, 1979 in Littlefield, Texas.

Ada Lee Ann Berry Rogers#

Ada Lee Berry was born July 11th 1875. On Sept 1, 1895 she married William Jackson Rogers. William J. Rogers was born in Granville County, NC on October 10th 1864. He was the son

of William Clinton Rogers and Rachel Starke Holeman.

item.title: Albert Edgar Berry
item.description:
Albert Edgar Berry, son of Jesse Lee and Nancy Ann Henry Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
  • Gen 4 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everett — Georgia
  • Gen 5 Jesse Lee Berry × Nancy Ann Henry — Georgia & Alabama
  • Gen 6 Albert Edgar Berry × Ella Claudia Dorton — Georgia

**L to R. Sarah Florence Berry Kelly, William Oscar Berry, Albert Edgar Berry, Addie Lena Berry Maners, Cora Mae Berry McVickers, Walter Glenn Berry is not shown in this picture Albert Edgar Berry **was born on December 28, 1881 in Etowah County Alabama. Albert died om May 23, 1964. He and his first wife are buried at Bethany Baptist Church Cemetery, Henry County, Georgia. Albert Edgar Berry and Ella Claudia Dorton were married on the 23 January 1905. She was born February 2, 1880 and died February 15 1916.

Cora Dena Berry

item.title: Dora Ellen Turley Ga
item.description:
Dora Ellen Turley, daughter of Nathan and Mary Jori Berry Turley#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry Berry
  5. Generation Mary Jori Berry Turley and Nathan Turley

DORA ELLEN TURLEY born April 11, 1874 in Milton County Georgia and died June 6, 1915 after giving birth to twin daughters. She married George Robert Pace before 1892. Dora Ellen Turley Pace is buried in the Holly Springs Cemetery, Cobb County Georgia.

item.title: Fiddleton 1891 Map
item.description:
Fiddleton — The 1891 Orange County Map

An 1891 map showing Berry family landmarks in northern Orange County, North Carolina.

Fiddleton Homestead, J.R. Berry Home, and Berry's Grove Baptist Church, circa 1891. Click to enlarge.

This is a map of northern Orange County circa 1891, published in 1940. It shows several Berry family landmarks, including John Robert Berry’s homestead, the Fiddleton Plantation, Berry’s Grove Church, and the Meridith Post Office, which was located inside John Robert Berry’s General Store (now located across the road from its original location).

item.title: Frederick M. Berry Iowa
item.description:
Frederick M. Berry (1874–1951)

Son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry, Linn County, Iowa, to Polk County, Wisconsin.

Frederick M. Berry, son of Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Joshua Berry Jr. and Anna Thorpe Berry: Ohio
  5. Generation Lewis and Elizabeth Haywood Berry: Indiana and Kansas
  6. Generation Harrison and Henrietta Havens Berry: Iowa and Colorado

FREDERICK M. BERRY was born 28 February 1874 in Palo, Linn County, Iowa to Harrison and Henrietta (Havens) Berry. When Fred was four years old in 1878, his mother Henrietta died. According to the newspaper article in the Vinton Eagle: “Her husband was cutting a large tree down close to where they lived, when Henrietta attempted to go near where he was to tell him that she was going to a neighbors, having her babe in her arms and a little girl running along with her, when the tree fell, crushing her almost into a jelly, while remarkable as it may seem, the baby still lives, though probably fatally injured. The little girl too, narrowly escaped.” RosaBelle did survive, but Henrietta died instantly and baby Eliza died within three days.

item.title: James Emory Taylor (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. James Emory Taylor
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Sarah Berry × Robert A. Taylor (3rd Husband) — Galveston, TX
Gen 7James Emory Taylor × Elizabeth Maculine — Rusk County, TX → Galveston, TX

Generation 7 James Emory Taylor

Born
c. 1866, Rusk County, Texas
Married
Elizabeth Maculine (b. May 1872; d. 1954)
Parents
Sarah Berry and Robert A. Taylor
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

James Emory Taylor

was born about 1866 in Rusk County Texas. On the left is a picture of James Emory Taylor’s mother Sarah Berry Taylor. James Emory Taylor married Elizabeth Maculine who was born in May 1872 and died in 1954. They had six children who would all be the seventh generation from Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry from Orange County North Carolina. The James E. Taylor Family lived in Galveston Texas.

item.title: Leona Berry Borden (TX)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Leona Berry Borden
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Robert Berry Jr. × Mary Waggoner — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 4George Berry × Susan Berry — NC → Fayette County, AL
Gen 5John Berry × Mary Howell — NC, AL → Rusk County, TX
Gen 6Alexander Howard Berry × Josephine Gregory — Texas
Gen 7Leona Berry × Ben Summers / Dick Simpson — Indian Territory → Borden County, TX

Generation 7 Leona Berry

Born
November 19, 1894, Indian Territory
Married
1st: Ben Summers (m. January 4, 1911; d. 1918) — 2nd: Montgomery 'Dick' Simpson (m. November 27, 1920)
Parents
Alexander Howard Berry and Josephine Priscilla Gregory Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Leona Berry was born on November 19, 1894 in Indian Territory. Leona Berry married Ben Summers Jan 4 1911. He died in the 1917 flu epidemic in Colorado. The family heard there was work in Colorado so the A.H. Berrys, the Willie Berrys with son Alec, daughter Hettie and a second son, Willie Howell Jr., born June 2, 1917, the Ben Summers family with three children, Newborn, born 1913, J.C., born 1915 and Maggie born 1917, the Hal Johnsons and seven children, Myrtle, Edith, Lula, Matthew, Albert, WillEtta, and Leola, and the C.L. Johnsons all loaded into covered wagons and went to Pueblo, Colorado. When they arrived at Pueblo, the Hal Johnson, and Ben Summers families loaded their belongings into boxcars and continued on to Rifle, Colorado. A.H. Berry was not well and decided to continue on in the wagons. He was accompanied by the Willie Berry family. The C.L. Johnson family stayed in Pueblo. Rifle was across the mountains from Pueblo and when A.H. and his group reached Salida, Colorado the altitude was too high and he could not breathe. They backed the wheels of the wagons into the river to turn around as the road was too narrow, and returned to Pueblo. When they arrived there was a message waiting for Willie from Hal Johnson asking them to wait as they were returning on the same train they had gone on. They had unloaded nothing but the horses. After they returned to Pueblo the group found jobs and went to work. In a few months A.H. became homesick and wanted to return to Texas. The A.H. Berrys and the Willie Berrys did so at that time, August 1918, but the Johnsons and Summers families did not return with them. Those who stayed in Colorado all came down with the influenza and Ben Summers died. So many people were dying that it was almost a week before he could be buried in Pueblo. A few months after Ben Summers death, Hal Johnson and his family and Leona (Onie) Berry Summers and her children loaded their livestock and belongings into a boxcar and rode the train back to O’Donnell. That was December 1918. When they returned to Texas they again settled in Borden County. Leona and her three children moved into the house with her parents.

On November 27, 1920 Leona (Onie) Summers married Montgomery (Dick) Simpson in Tahoka, Texas.

item.title: Mary A. Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mary A. Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Frances Bowling — Orange County, NC
Gen 6Mary A. Berry × James G. Holeman / James Ormond Webb — Orange County, NC

Generation 6 Mary A. Berry

Born
May 18, 1873
Died
May 23, 1963
Married
1st: James G. Holeman (married June 25, 1896) — 2nd: James Ormond Webb (married January 27, 1904)
Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Mary A. Berry#

Mary W. Berry was born May 18, 1873 and died in May 23, 1963 (1900, 1910, 1920 Orange County Censuses). Mary W. Holeman is listed living in the home of her father John Robert Berry in 1900 Census. We have recently found that Mary W. Berry was first married to James G. Holeman on June 25th 1896. He was the son of James and Emma B. Holeman from Orange County, NC. Her second marriage was to James Ormond Webb on January 27th 1904. In the 1910 census she was listed as the wife of James O. Webb and they had a 5 year old son named James O. Webb Jr. James Ormond Webb Sr. was born in 1859 and died in June 19th 1936. His parents were Sidney Smith Webb and Elizabeth Ellen Morrow. Both Jim and Mary Berry Webb are buried at Bethleham Presbyterian Church cemetery in Orange County Cemetery, Orange County, NC. There were only two children born to this union.

item.title: Mary Elizabeth Berry NC
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Mary Elizabeth Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Bowling — Orange County, NC
Gen 6Wiley P. Berry × Ida Ann Wilson — Orange County, NC
Gen 7Mary Elizabeth Berry × Brodie Carr — Orange County, NC → Hillsborough, NC

Generation 7 Mary Elizabeth Berry

Born
August 23, 1899, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
November 13, 1957, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Brodie Carr (married September 17, 1925)
Parents
Wiley P. Berry and Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Mary Elizabeth Berry#

MARY ELIZABETH BERRY was named for her two grandmothers Mary Jane Coleman Wilson and Elizabeth Frances Bowling Berry. She was born August 23, 1899 and she was married on September 17, 1925. Aunt Maye died with a rare type of cancer on November 13, 1957, at the age of 58.

Gen 6Mary Lou Turley × John Thomas Hartsfield / John England — Milton County, Georgia

Born
c. 1872, Milton County, Georgia
Died
1958, buried at Harmony Grove Baptist Church Cemetery, Cobb County, Georgia
Married

Parents
Nathan Turley and Mary Jori Berry

item.title: Nathaniel Arie Turley Ga
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Nathaniel Arie Turley
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3David J. Berry × Mary Blalock — Orange County, NC → Georgia
Gen 4Robert Berry × Elizabeth Everitt Berry — Georgia
Gen 5Mary Jori Berry × Nathan Turley — Milton County, Georgia
Gen 6Nathaniel Arie Turley — Milton County, Georgia

Generation 6 Nathaniel Arie Turley

Born
c. 1873, Milton County, Georgia
Died
1937, buried in Ponder Cemetery
Married
1st: Mettie A. Haley (married November 25, 1900) — 2nd: Nancy B. Garrison (married January 6, 1926)
Parents
Nathan Turley and Mary Jori Berry
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

NATHANIEL ARIE TURLEY was born about 1873 in Milton County Georgia. He died in 1937 and was buried in Ponder Cemetery. Arie, as he was known by the family, was married twice. He married Mettie A. Haley on November 25, 1900. When Mettie died, Arie married Nancy B. Garrison on January 6, 1926. There were no children born to either of these unions.

item.title: Page Updates
item.description:
Site Update History

A running log of new pages, content additions, and research updates. Latest entries are at the top.

If you have pictures or information about your family line, we’d love to hear from you through our Contact page.

  • 4/04/2026 Created James Thomas Wilson page and updated Pattie Berry Wilson page

item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors

Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
Gen 4Thomas Person Berry × Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
Gen 5John Robert Berry × Elizabeth Francis Bowling — Orange County, NC
Gen 6Pattie Swiney Berry × Felix Curry Wilson — Orange County, NC

Generation 6 Pattie Swiney Berry

Born
August 8, 1886, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
December 12, 1915, Red Springs, Robeson County, North Carolina
Married
Felix Curry 'Bob' Wilson (married December 20, 1910)
Parents
John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Francis Bowling, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Pattie Swiney Berry#

PATTIE SWINEY BERRY was born on August 8, 1886, in Orange County, North Carolina. She

only lived for 29 years because she died on December 12, 1915, in Red Springs, Robeson County, NC. She is buried at Berry’s Grove Baptist Church Cemetery, Little River Township, Orange County, NC.

The Story of Walter Glen Berry#

item.title: William Aaron Turley Ga
item.description:
William Aaron Turley (1879–1960)

Son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley, Georgia and Alabama.

William Aaron Turley, son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley#

Ancestors#

  • Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina
  • Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry: NC and Georgia
  • Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry: Georgia
  • Generation Mary Jori Berry and Nathan Turley: Alabama

WILLIAM AARON TURLEY born August 26, 1879 and died November 6, 1960. William Aaron Turley was the youngest child of Nathan and Mary J. Berry Turley. He was born on August 26th, 1876 according to his tombstone, however on the 1880 census he is listed as six months old. Since he left home at an early age he may have added three years to his age.

item.title: William Aaron Turley Ga
item.description:
William Aaron Turley

Son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley, Georgia and Alabama.

William Aaron Turley, son of Mary J. Berry and Nathan Turley#

ANCESTORS

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry Orange County, North Carolina
  3. Generation David J. and Mary Blalock Berry: NC and Georgia
  4. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Everitt Berry Georgia
  5. Generation Mary Jori Berry and Nathan Turley Alabama

WILLIAM AARON TURLEY born August 26 1879 and died November 6, 1960. William Aaron Turley was the youngest child of Nathan and Mary J. Berry Turley. He was born on August 26th 1876 according to his tombstone, however on the 1880 census he is listed as six months old. Since he left home

at an early age he may have added three years to his age. It is not known exactly where he wandered before he finally settled in Alabama about 1908. The picture on the right is of Nathaniel Arie (on the left) and William Aaron Turley On the (right).Nathaniel Aire Berry is Will’s brother.This Picture was taken in the early 1920’s. .We know that William lived both in Blount and Morgan Counties Alabama before he married Emma Allena Berry. Emma who is the daughter of Robert Riley and Martha “Mattie” Stark Berry. Will, as he was known in Alabama, and Emma Turley had four children. Melvin Ray Berry died at birth.

item.title: William Edgar Berry Alabama
item.description:
William Edgar Berry

Son of Albert Edgar and Ella Clania Berry, Alabama.

Generation 1. Robert and Mary Williamson Berry Princess Anne County, VA.

Generation 2. Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry Orange County, NC

Generation 3. David J. and Mary Blaylock Berry Orange County, NC and Georgia.

item.title: William Eugene Berry NC
item.description:
William Eugene Berry (1896–1984)

Son of Wiley P. and Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.

William Eugene Berry, was the son of Wiley P. Berry Sr#

ANCESTORS

  1. Generation: Robert and Mary Williamson Berry, Princess Anne County Virginia
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation William and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  4. Generation Thomas Person and Sarah Lunsford Berry,Orange County, North Carolina.
  5. Generation John Robert and Elizabeth Bowling Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.
  6. Generation Wiley P and Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Orange County, North Carolina.

WILLIAM EUGENE BERRY was born on September 1st 1896 and died on September 30th 1984. He married Pattie Alma McAdams on June 6th 1924. Alma as she was known by the family was born November 17th 1899 and died April 16th 1993. Gene Berry spent time in the Army during World War One. His picture in uniform is on the left This picture was probably taken around Christmas 1918
